raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I was expecting delivery of an item purchased through XXXX for {$3900.00}. The XXXX tracking later showed the package as delivered, however I never received the item. 
After contacting XXXX, the delivery driver admitted that on XX/XX/year> he handed my package to an unknown individual who was walking on the sidewalk near my residence. The individual was not me, did not live at my address, and the identification did not match my name or delivery address. Despite this, the driver allowed the individual to sign for and take possession of my package. 

XXXX conducted an internal investigation and interview with the driver. In a written email, XXXX management confirmed that the driver admitted to misdelivering the package to a random person and stated that the driver was disciplined for failing to follow delivery and identification procedures. 

Because the tracking shows delivered, XXXX has denied my refund request despite the item never being received. XXXX has also declined reimbursement beyond the {$100.00} insurance limit. Barclays has denied my dispute based solely on the delivery scan, despite written confirmation from XXXX that the package was misdelivered and lost. 

The item was never received by me, and the carrier has provided written confirmation that delivery was made in error to an unauthorized individual. 
Barclays denied my billing dispute solely based on a delivery scan, despite written confirmation from XXXX management that the package was lost/misdelivered and never received. I contacted the carrier as instructed and provided their written admission of loss. Barclays is refusing to consider the carriers final determination and is incorrectly shifting responsibility back to the carrier after fulfillment failure.
